body.title: Seed SWADL software-design concepts as FACTUAL nodes in Igor's cortex
body.result: 5 SWADL concept anchors deposited + embedded (768-dim). SEPARATION_OF_CONCERNS (CP1, 0.8) via Igor's own store_memory call. PAGE_OBJECT/FLOW_OBJECT/IDEMPOTENCE (CP1, 0.7) and SELECTOR (CP3, 0.6) authored by Igor but dispatched by CC when Igor printed the calls as code blocks rather than invoking them (LLM tool-dispatch miss, not tool-surface issue). Narratives are in Igor's voice. Provenance: source='swadl_concepts_draft.md' on the first, 'igor_self_authored_2026_04_19' on the rest. Root cause of the SWADL block was graph_write.py not accepting memory_id/tags/identity_weight/source — shipped in commit 288988ef. Next: T-gmail-login-page-object can now reference these anchors.
body.description:
Introduce SWADL's core concepts to Igor's graph so they become available for co-activation with future site-specific work. Collaborative — CC explains, Igor deposits via normal memory pipeline (so the nodes carry his own salience/interpretation, not just CC's handoff).

## Concepts to seed
- PAGE_OBJECT — a stable abstraction over a single UI surface; hides selectors; exposes actions
- FLOW_OBJECT — sequence of page-object actions representing a domain task (e.g. login, send email)
- SELECTOR — the brittle string that finds an element; lives inside page objects, not flows
- IDEMPOTENCE — a good flow can be run twice without breaking
- SEPARATION_OF_CONCERNS — UI changes touch page objects only; domain logic lives in flows

## Shape
- Each concept is a FACTUAL memory node with identity_weight=0.7 (high — these are anchors)
- Edges: MEANING_TO_ME from each concept back to 'software_design' parent
- Co-activation edges wired manually between related concepts (PAGE_OBJECT ↔ SELECTOR, FLOW_OBJECT ↔ PAGE_OBJECT)
- Igor reads CC's explanation, does his own interpretation, produces the deposit — NOT CC dictating node text

## Related
- Parent: T-igor-as-user-epic
- Unblocks: T-gmail-login-page-object
